entities.name: TOP SOIL
entities.description: Nutrient-rich upper layer of soil essential for sustaining agricultural productivity

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of land to produce crops, which is constrained by soil erosion

entities.name: HILLY TERRAINS
entities.description: Sloping land areas where soil erosion is a serious crop production constraint

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of topsoil, which deteriorates the ecology of hilly terrains

entities.name: SOUTHERN CHINA
entities.description: Region where soil erosion is a serious issue and pigeonpea was implemented

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume used to protect fragile lands and arrest soil erosion

entities.name: LATE MATURING
entities.description: A specific characteristic of pigeonpea cultivars used for soil protection

entities.name: GUANGXI
entities.description: Province in China where late maturing pigeonpea was used to protect fragile lands

entities.name: YUNNAN
entities.description: Province in China where late maturing pigeonpea was used to protect fragile lands

entities.name: SOIL CONSERVATION PRACTICES
entities.description: Methodologies involving the use of pigeonpea to arrest soil erosion

relationships.description: Conservation of top soil is important for sustaining agricultural productivity
Soil erosion acts as a serious crop production constraint
Soil erosion is particularly severe in sloping hilly terrains
Soil erosion is a serious issue in southern China
Pigeonpea is used to arrest soil erosion
Pigeonpea was used to protect fragile lands in Guangxi
Pigeonpea was used to protect fragile lands in Yunnan
Late maturing cultivars of pigeonpea were specifically used
Soil conservation practices involve the use of pigeonpea
Soil conservation practices are used to arrest soil erosion
